preview_text:
The model favors England at 63%, with Panama's win probability at 12%. England enters as clear favorite, but the model assigns Panama better than one-in-ten chances—a meaningful underdog floor reflecting the inherent variance in knockout football.

The 63–12–25 spread points to a competitive match tilted toward England but not overwhelmingly so. A draw sits at nearly one-quarter probability, indicating the model expects close play where either side could force extra time. The gap between the top two outcomes is substantial enough to make England the likely winner, yet the distribution suggests Panama retains realistic paths to advance.

The sportsbook consensus diverges materially from the model. Books price England at 81%, compared to the model's 63%—an 18-point gap—while Panama drops to 7% and draws compress to 12%. Polymarket aligns closer to sportsbooks than the model. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is more accurate; the divergence itself merits attention as meaningful disagreement among forecast sources.
